raw.comments: 14615269141 sammula command to open waves:  nfs/site/disks/corioh_emu_usr_002/bdasari/COR_IOH/emu_build/ww11a/dmrhub2-a0-corioh-26ww13a_rev0/verif/emu/scripts/waveforms/auto-iCSA.csh /nfs/site/disks/xpg_dmrhub_0355/sdabhade/COR-IOH/model-build/ww13a/regression/cohcollider.5/fullchip_trace.ztdb rc file: /nfs/site/disks/xpg_dmrhub_0355/sdabhade/rc_file/cor_ioh/ph3_soc_infra.rc  22611824021 ssmahade Looks like the config spec has below line commented out: /nfs/site/disks/dmr_fe_mod_0014/dmrhub2/dmrhub2-a0-corioh-26ww13a/die_ioh/fabric/config/config_utils.py #IOH_FIXME#    soc.search("u_ns_socb_pcg_parsocsouth0chassis1_pcg1").attrs["rst"] = "s5_crux" This is causing the automation to pick the default reset of "S0 crux" causing the SOC connection to be tied to S0 instead of S5.  @Kona, Siva N  working on fix.  1667411523 swacheta For this issue, Vijay has updated the die_ioh/fabric/config/config_utils.py and turnin is done. Fix is in TOT. Thanks

articles.list_title: [COR IOH EMU] [ww 13a]:  HWRS sending out imh2cbb hwsync  even though imh2cbb hwsync connected is 0x0
articles.subject: hsdes_bugeco

raw.title: [COR IOH EMU] [ww 13a]:  HWRS sending out imh2cbb hwsync  even though imh2cbb hwsync connected is 0x0
raw.description: current HWRS has  following logic that sends out  imh2cbb hwsync req out messages -  for IOH CBBs are disabled. observations 1. HWRS drives CBB_DIE_READY                          2. HWRS still sends imh2cbb reqs on d2d  - either tie them off to 0x1                           3. soc is tying cbb ipready to 0xf  -  [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/14027683355]  assign imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual = (imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_sync) | (~hwrs_treg_registers.SKU_FEATURE_DWORD1.imh2cbb_hwsync_connected)   imh2cbb_hwsync_req_out <= (hwrs_final_die_id=='h8)? (&imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual & hwrs_treg_registers.HWRS_DRIVE_PINS_GROUP0_3.PHASE_3_CBB_DIE_READY) : (imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual[0] & imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual[1] & hwrs_treg_registers.HWRS_DRIVE_PINS_GROUP0_3.PHASE_3_CBB_DIE_READY);
raw.subject: bugeco
raw.comments: 1667447937 mitalija Hi Sandeep,Just got the confirmation that ww16 hwrs ip uprev is done. Can you rerun with this.  Feel free to reopen hsd, in case you still see an issue. Thanks, Mitali

raw.description: current HWRS has  following logic that sends out  imh2cbb hwsync req out messages -  for IOH CBBs are disabled. observations 1. HWRS drives CBB_DIE_READY                          2. HWRS still sends imh2cbb reqs on d2d  - either tie them off to 0x1                           3. soc is tying cbb ipready to 0xf  -  [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/14027683365]  assign imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual = (imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_sync) | (~hwrs_treg_registers.SKU_FEATURE_DWORD1.imh2cbb_hwsync_connected)   imh2cbb_hwsync_req_out <= (hwrs_final_die_id=='h8)? (&imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual & hwrs_treg_registers.HWRS_DRIVE_PINS_GROUP0_3.PHASE_3_CBB_DIE_READY) : (imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual[0] & imh2cbb_hwsync_req_in_qual[1] & hwrs_treg_registers.HWRS_DRIVE_PINS_GROUP0_3.PHASE_3_CBB_DIE_READY);
raw.subject: bugeco

articles.list_title: COR-IOH: Hitting an error for prime-code tracker generation in IOH
articles.subject: hsdes_bugeco

raw.title: COR-IOH: Hitting an error for prime-code tracker generation in IOH
raw.description:
Normal 0 false false false EN-US X-NONE X-NONE MicrosoftInternetExplorer4 /* Style Definitions */ table.MsoNormalTable {mso-style-name:"Table Normal"; mso-tstyle-rowband-size:0; mso-tstyle-colband-size:0; mso-style-noshow:yes; mso-style-priority:99; mso-style-parent:""; mso-padding-alt:0in 5.4pt 0in 5.4pt; mso-para-margin:0in; mso-pagination:widow-orphan; font-size:10.0pt; font-family:"Times New Roman",serif;} Hitting an error for prime-code tracker generation in IOH. Path for run: /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/aksonava/corioh_model_ww17_1_a/regression/ioh/ioh_full/imh_FULL_FFP.list/imh_base_test_FFP_TPMI_INIT command used:   setenv PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234 $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser Path
for run: /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/aksonava/corioh_model_ww17_1_a/regression/ioh/ioh_full/imh_FULL_FFP.list/imh_base_test_FFP_TPMI_INIT 

   

 command
used:   

 
 setenv
 PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT
 /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234 
 

 
 $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser
 --xtrace_file xtmp_trace.out --dis_file
 $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/runtime.fw.lst
 --gdb_debug_file
 $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/gdb_debug_data_rt.rb --dut=cor_ioh
 --trace_type turbo --model_root $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT >
 primecode_runtime_tracker-new.tmp 
 

   

 Hitting
below error: 

 /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser:445:in
`initialize': No such file or directory @ rb_sysopen - xtmp_trace.out
(Errno::ENOENT) 

  
      from
/nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser:445:in
`open' 

  
      from
/nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser:445:in
`parse_trace_streaming' 

  
      from
/nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser:196:in
`run' 

  
      from
/nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser:983:in
`<main>'     --dis_file $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/runtime.fw.lst --gdb_debug_file $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/gdb_debug_data_rt.rb --dut=cor_ioh --trace_type turbo --model_root $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT > primecode_runtime_tracker-new.tmp Please let me know if command need any changes here for IOH.
raw.subject: bugeco
raw.comments: 14615374528 kadavis2 error is related to '--xtrace_file xtmp_trace.out', xtmp_trace.out is not found in the directory that command was run in.  FYI parser upgrades will use the '--dis_file' path to find the other model_root files. the options '--trace_type', '--gdb_debug_file', '--dut', and '--model_root' are optional. they override what autodetect calculated. --out was added to direct to a file. adding '.gz' to the --out filename will create gzip-compressed output. recommend trying: Path for run: /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/aksonava/corioh_model_ww17_1_a/regression/ioh/ioh_full/imh_FULL_FFP.list/imh_base_test_FFP_TPMI_INIT   command used:   setenv PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT /nfs/site/disks/corioh_fe_val_rcf_002/IOH_PRIMECODE_IMAGES/primecode-2026ww16-5234 $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser --xtrace_file $PATH_TO_RUN/xtmp_trace.out   --dis_file $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/runtime.fw.lst  > primecode_runtime_tracker-new.tmp $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/tools/fw_trace_parser/bin/fw_trace_parser --xtrace_file $PATH_TO_RUN/xtmp_trace.out   --dis_file $PRIMECODE_MODEL_ROOT/target/cor_ioh/pcode/bin/runtime.fw.lst --out=primecode_runtime_tracker-new.log.gz  14615393507 kadavis2 https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/14027743478 Joe Brooks was able to generate a FW Trace log using the parser. Closing ww20.5 unless more information provided.
